narrative_summary:
The BRCA1 missense variant N1647K (c.4941C>A) has been examined in the context of assessing variants of uncertain significance in breast‑cancer susceptibility genes. Although the variant resides in the BRCT domain of BRCA1, no specific disease association or clinical classification is provided in the extracted literature.

Functional assays have incorporated N1647K. One study reported that the variant was retested after earlier variable results, but the excerpt does not disclose the outcome of that retesting [PMC5539989]. A second investigation listed N1647K among 117 BRCT missense variants evaluated by structural and transcriptional activity assays, yet the snippet does not specify whether the variant showed a functional effect, moderate effect, or no effect [PMC3040717]. Consequently, the functional impact of N1647K remains undefined in the available evidence.

Because no clinical case data, population frequency information, or computational predictions are presented, and the functional results are not detailed, there is insufficient evidence to assign a definitive pathogenic or benign classification. The current data leave the N1647K variant as a variant of uncertain significance pending further functional and clinical investigation.

mentions.quoted_text: We retrieved all 84 BRCA1 missense variants deposited the BIC database (http://research.nhgri.nih.gov/bic/) that had not been analysed in the TA assay (Supplementary Table S2). In addition, we tested five novel variants: S1486C (HGVS c.4456A>T), S1580Y (HGVS c.4739C>A), C1697Y (HGVS c.5090G>A), H1746Y (c.5236C>T) and L1844P (HGVS c.5531T>C). We also retested nine variants **N1647K** (HGVS **c.4941C>A**), V1696L (HGVS c.5086G>C), G1706E (HGVS c.5117G>A), M1783T (HGVS c.5348T>C), G1788D (HGVS c.5363G>A), A1823T (HGVS c.5467G>A), L1844P (HGVS c.5531T>C), V1833M (HGVS c.5497G>A) and C1787S (HGVS c.5360G>C) that had displayed variable results in previous tests.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study evaluates functional assays for BRCA1 VUS and notes that the N1647K variant was retested, but the functional result for this variant is not detailed in the excerpt.
article_mentions.title: Functional assays provide a robust tool for the clinical annotation of genetic variants of uncertain significance

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study performed structural and functional assays on 117 BRCA1 BRCT missense variants, including N1647K, to classify their pathogenic potential, but the specific functional impact of N1647K is not detailed in the extracted snippet.
article_mentions.title: Comprehensive Analysis of Missense Variations in the BRCT Domain of BRCA1 by Structural and Functional Assays
